schema_notes.purpose: Actionable MVP blueprint library for product types. Each requirement is written as a concrete question the system should answer before building.

minimum_requirements.label: who supplies
minimum_requirements.question: Who are the providers/sellers that list on the platform?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Without a clear supply side, the marketplace has nothing to show or match.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Provider type, what they offer, what information they must submit, and how they are approved.

minimum_requirements.label: who buys
minimum_requirements.question: Who are the users/customers searching or requesting offers?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The buyer side determines discovery, filters, messaging, and payment flow.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Customer segment, the problem they are solving, and the fields needed to match them to offers.

minimum_requirements.label: listing page
minimum_requirements.question: What minimum information must every listing show?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Users cannot compare offers if listing data is inconsistent or incomplete.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Title, short description, price or pricing method, location, availability, and one trust signal.

minimum_requirements.label: match or request flow
minimum_requirements.question: What is the exact action that starts a transaction?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The MVP must make one core interaction work end to end.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Search or browse flow, one clear CTA, and what happens after the click.

minimum_requirements.label: trust layer
minimum_requirements.question: What is the minimum trust or moderation needed before launch?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Marketplaces fail quickly if users do not trust providers or if low-quality listings go live.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Manual approval, identity check, review rule, fraud check, or content moderation rule.

minimum_requirements.label: money flow
minimum_requirements.question: Who pays whom, when, and how does the platform earn money?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The marketplace business model depends on timing and fee structure.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Buyer payment timing, provider payout timing, and platform revenue rule.

minimum_requirements.label: admin control
minimum_requirements.question: What must the internal team track every day to keep the marketplace running?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A marketplace needs operational visibility even in an MVP.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: At least one admin queue for pending providers, flagged listings, requests, and payment issues.

blueprints.product_type_description: A platform that connects sellers/providers with buyers/customers and helps the transaction happen.

minimum_requirements.label: bookable service
minimum_requirements.question: What exactly can a customer book?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The product is unclear if the appointment unit is not defined.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Service/session name, duration, delivery format, and what the customer gets.

minimum_requirements.label: availability source
minimum_requirements.question: Where do available slots come from?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The booking flow breaks if there is no reliable source of truth for time slots.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Who sets availability, how often it changes, and whether booking is automatic or reviewed.

minimum_requirements.label: booking flow
minimum_requirements.question: What are the exact steps from choosing a slot to confirmation?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The MVP must make booking easy with no ambiguous decisions.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Slot selection, customer details, booking confirmation, and status rule.

minimum_requirements.label: customer intake
minimum_requirements.question: What information is required before the appointment?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Providers need the right information to deliver the service well.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Required fields only; do not collect extra data just because it might be useful later.

minimum_requirements.label: changes and no-shows
minimum_requirements.question: How are cancellations, reschedules, and no-shows handled?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Even a simple booking product needs a basic policy to avoid chaos.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Cancellation window, reschedule rule, and no-show consequence.

minimum_requirements.label: payment timing
minimum_requirements.question: When does the customer pay?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Booking products often fail if payment and confirmation are not clearly linked.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Before, after, deposit-only, or free booking with later payment.

minimum_requirements.label: delivery handoff
minimum_requirements.question: How does the booked service actually happen?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A booking product is incomplete if there is no operational handoff to delivery.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Meeting link, address, calendar invite, reminders, and provider notification.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business where customers reserve a time slot for a service or session.

minimum_requirements.label: single job to be done
minimum_requirements.question: What is the one main problem the product solves first?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A SaaS MVP becomes bloated if it tries to solve many jobs at once.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One user problem stated as a clear action or outcome.

minimum_requirements.label: primary user role
minimum_requirements.question: Who is the main person using the product day to day?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The main user determines screens, permissions, and wording.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One primary role and one optional secondary role.

minimum_requirements.label: must-work workflow
minimum_requirements.question: What is the one workflow that must work end to end?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: This is the real MVP boundary.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Start state, user actions, and end result.

minimum_requirements.label: core records
minimum_requirements.question: What minimum data objects must the product store?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The data model should support the workflow, not a future roadmap.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: List of essential records and the fields that matter most.

minimum_requirements.label: essential screens
minimum_requirements.question: What screens are absolutely required for the workflow?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Without a minimum screen list, the build scope grows too fast.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Only the pages needed for the core workflow.

minimum_requirements.label: account and access
minimum_requirements.question: How do users enter the product and what can they do?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Even simple software needs a clear access model.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Signup/login rule, password reset, and whether multiple roles exist.

minimum_requirements.label: trust baseline
minimum_requirements.question: What minimum reliability and support setup is needed?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: B2B users will not trust the product without a basic safety net.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Backup rule, error logging, and one support channel.

blueprints.product_type_description: Software people or companies use online to solve a specific problem, usually through a browser or app.

minimum_requirements.label: developer use case
minimum_requirements.question: What technical problem does the API solve?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A developer platform must be built around one integration job, not broad promises.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One developer problem and the systems it plugs into.

minimum_requirements.label: API contract
minimum_requirements.question: What request comes in and what response comes out?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The product is the contract itself.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Inputs, outputs, authentication method, and major error cases.

minimum_requirements.label: minimum endpoints
minimum_requirements.question: Which endpoints are required for the MVP?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Too many endpoints make the first version harder to ship and document.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Only the few endpoints needed to deliver the core use case.

minimum_requirements.label: access model
minimum_requirements.question: How do developers get credentials and authenticate calls?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Access must be simple and secure enough to use.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: API key, token, or account model; how credentials are issued.

minimum_requirements.label: documentation
minimum_requirements.question: What minimum docs are required for self-serve use?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Without docs, the product is not really usable by developers.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Quickstart, authentication instructions, example request, example response, and error explanation.

minimum_requirements.label: limits and monitoring
minimum_requirements.question: What protects the API from abuse or unexpected load?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: APIs need basic operational protection from day one.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Rate limits, request logging, latency/error tracking.

minimum_requirements.label: pricing or quota
minimum_requirements.question: How will usage be limited or monetized?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Even if the MVP is free, there must be a clear usage rule.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Free quota, pay-per-use, or account-based plan.

blueprints.product_type_description: A product that other software developers integrate into their own apps or systems.

minimum_requirements.label: decision supported
minimum_requirements.question: What decision or insight does the product help the user make?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Analytics products are valuable only if they help users act.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One important decision or monitoring problem.

minimum_requirements.label: data inputs
minimum_requirements.question: What data sources are required for the MVP?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The value of the product depends on usable inputs.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Exact data sources and how they get into the system.

minimum_requirements.label: core outputs
minimum_requirements.question: What charts, scores, reports, or alerts must the user receive?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The MVP should focus on a small set of outputs people care about.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: 5-10 key metrics or one recurring report type.

minimum_requirements.label: refresh method
minimum_requirements.question: How often does the product update and by what method?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Users need to know whether they are seeing fresh numbers or static reports.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Manual upload, daily sync, weekly refresh, etc.

minimum_requirements.label: explanation layer
minimum_requirements.question: How will the product explain where numbers come from?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Trust is crucial in analytics products.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Metric definitions, source references, or formulas.

minimum_requirements.label: user workflow
minimum_requirements.question: What does the user do after seeing the analysis?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The product should support action, not just display data.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Filter, compare, export, share, or trigger alerts.

minimum_requirements.label: data quality checks
minimum_requirements.question: What minimum validation protects users from bad analysis?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Analytics products are dangerous if they silently use broken inputs.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Missing fields, duplicates, date mismatches, obvious anomalies.

blueprints.product_type_description: A product that collects, processes, or presents data so users can monitor or make decisions.

minimum_requirements.label: manual process replaced
minimum_requirements.question: What manual workflow is being reduced or automated?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The MVP should target one painful internal process.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Current process, owner, and where time is lost.

minimum_requirements.label: trigger event
minimum_requirements.question: What starts the workflow?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Automation needs a clear beginning.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One trigger such as form submission, email, payment, or status change.

minimum_requirements.label: automation steps
minimum_requirements.question: What exact steps should happen automatically?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The product is the sequence, not a vague promise of automation.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Ordered steps and expected result.

minimum_requirements.label: human review points
minimum_requirements.question: Where must a human approve or correct the process?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Most internal tools still need human checkpoints.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Approval rule, exception cases, and manual override.

minimum_requirements.label: required data
minimum_requirements.question: What fields does the workflow need to run correctly?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The automation fails if critical fields are missing.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Only the required fields for routing and completion.

minimum_requirements.label: ops queue
minimum_requirements.question: How do humans see pending, blocked, or failed work?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Internal tools need visibility for exceptions.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: At least one dashboard or work queue.

minimum_requirements.label: failure handling
minimum_requirements.question: What happens when the automation cannot complete a step?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: No automation is complete without a fallback path.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Retry rule, manual review path, and error log.

blueprints.product_type_description: A tool that automates repeated business tasks or helps a team run internal processes.

minimum_requirements.label: daily user problem
minimum_requirements.question: What repeat consumer problem does the app solve?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A consumer MVP needs one obvious reason to exist.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One everyday problem or habit the user cares about.

minimum_requirements.label: main session action
minimum_requirements.question: What is the single most important thing a user does in one session?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: This defines the core UI and retention loop.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One action that creates immediate value.

minimum_requirements.label: first-use win
minimum_requirements.question: What must happen in the first two minutes so the app feels useful?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: If first-use value is weak, users will not come back.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: A fast success moment with no unnecessary setup.

minimum_requirements.label: repeat-use trigger
minimum_requirements.question: Why will the user come back tomorrow or next week?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Consumer apps need a reason to repeat.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Notification, saved progress, fresh content, streak, or new recommendations.

minimum_requirements.label: minimum content or data
minimum_requirements.question: What content or user data is needed to make the app useful?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The app needs enough substance to deliver value.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Only the minimum content/data needed for the first version.

minimum_requirements.label: monetization test
minimum_requirements.question: How will you test whether users will pay or accept monetization?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Even if monetization is early-stage, the MVP should not ignore it completely.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Premium feature, subscription gate, ads, or paid upgrade hypothesis.

minimum_requirements.label: support inside the app
minimum_requirements.question: How does a user report a problem or ask for help?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Support is part of trust even in a lightweight consumer app.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: At least one visible support or feedback path.

blueprints.product_type_description: A consumer app used directly by end users, usually on mobile or web.

minimum_requirements.label: community focus
minimum_requirements.question: What shared identity, topic, or goal brings members together?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Communities fail when the focus is broad or generic.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One audience and one reason to gather.

minimum_requirements.label: core interaction
minimum_requirements.question: What do members mainly do with each other?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The platform needs one interaction loop that creates activity.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Post, comment, chat, upvote, join event, share resource, etc.

minimum_requirements.label: membership rule
minimum_requirements.question: Who can join and how open is access?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Join rules affect trust, moderation, and growth.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Open signup, approval, invite-only, or verified categories.

minimum_requirements.label: content unit
minimum_requirements.question: What is the basic thing users create or respond to?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The platform needs a clear object around which activity happens.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Post, thread, event, question, profile, challenge, etc.

minimum_requirements.label: moderation baseline
minimum_requirements.question: What minimum rules keep the platform safe and usable?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Communities need moderation earlier than many founders expect.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Report flow, admin actions, and key banned behaviors.

minimum_requirements.label: trust signals
minimum_requirements.question: How do users know who is credible or safe to engage with?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Trust matters in social products.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Verification, profile completeness, badges, reputation, or role labeling.

minimum_requirements.label: health metric
minimum_requirements.question: How will you know the community is alive, not just signed up?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Social products need activity metrics, not only registration counts.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One or two engagement metrics.

blueprints.product_type_description: A platform where users interact, post, discuss, and build a network or group.

minimum_requirements.label: recurring value
minimum_requirements.question: What repeated value justifies paying again next month?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Recurring products need a continuing promise, not one-time value.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One ongoing benefit delivered each cycle.

minimum_requirements.label: membership scope
minimum_requirements.question: What exactly does a paying member get access to?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Users need a clear boundary between free and paid value.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Content, tools, discounts, community access, support, or physical delivery.

minimum_requirements.label: join flow
minimum_requirements.question: What are the exact steps from visitor to active member?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Membership products need a clear activation path.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Plan selection, payment, account creation, and access delivery.

minimum_requirements.label: billing rule
minimum_requirements.question: How and when are repeat payments collected?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The business model depends on predictable billing logic.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Monthly/yearly cadence, auto-renew or manual renew, failed payment handling.

minimum_requirements.label: access control
minimum_requirements.question: How do you enforce what paid and unpaid users can access?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Membership value leaks if access is not controlled.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Account rule, content gating, or membership status check.

minimum_requirements.label: retention system
minimum_requirements.question: What reduces churn after the first payment?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Retention is the real challenge in subscription businesses.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Fresh value, reminders, community activity, progress tracking, or support.

minimum_requirements.label: membership dashboard
minimum_requirements.question: What numbers do you need to track every week?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Subscriptions need visibility on starts, churn, and engagement.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: New members, active members, churn, failed payments, and usage.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business where users pay regularly to keep access to content, benefits, or services.

minimum_requirements.label: niche and audience
minimum_requirements.question: Who is the audience and what topic do you consistently cover?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Media products need a narrow niche to grow and monetize.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One audience + one topic promise.

minimum_requirements.label: content format
minimum_requirements.question: What format will you publish most consistently?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: One primary format is easier to execute and brand.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Newsletter, article, podcast, short video, long video, report, etc.

minimum_requirements.label: publishing cadence
minimum_requirements.question: How often will you publish?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Consistency matters more than ambition.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One realistic schedule.

minimum_requirements.label: distribution channels
minimum_requirements.question: Where will people discover and consume the content?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A media business needs a repeatable audience acquisition path.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One primary channel and optional secondary channel.

minimum_requirements.label: editorial workflow
minimum_requirements.question: How does a content piece move from idea to publication?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Without a workflow, consistency breaks down.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Idea collection, drafting, editing, publishing, and distribution.

minimum_requirements.label: audience capture point
minimum_requirements.question: Where does an anonymous viewer become a known subscriber or lead?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Media value compounds only if you can retain the audience.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Signup form, follow button, SMS opt-in, or account creation.

minimum_requirements.label: monetization path
minimum_requirements.question: What is the first realistic revenue stream?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Media businesses need an early revenue hypothesis.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Sponsorship, paid membership, premium report, affiliate, lead generation, etc.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business built around attracting an audience and monetizing attention.

minimum_requirements.label: digital product sold
minimum_requirements.question: What exactly is the buyer purchasing?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The offer must be concrete, not just 'content'.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One named digital asset or bundle.

minimum_requirements.label: buyer outcome
minimum_requirements.question: What result does the buyer get after using it?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Digital products sell outcomes, not files.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One practical result.

minimum_requirements.label: delivery method
minimum_requirements.question: How does the buyer receive access after purchase?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The purchase-to-access flow must be instant and reliable.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Download, email, account access, or shared folder.

minimum_requirements.label: sales proof
minimum_requirements.question: What helps a buyer trust the digital product is useful?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Digital products are easy to doubt before purchase.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Preview, screenshots, samples, testimonials, or demo.

minimum_requirements.label: checkout flow
minimum_requirements.question: What are the exact steps from landing page to access?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A digital product MVP still needs a clean purchase flow.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Page, checkout, payment, confirmation, access.

minimum_requirements.label: usage rights
minimum_requirements.question: What is the buyer allowed or not allowed to do with the product?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Simple rights rules reduce confusion and misuse.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Single-user use, no resale, team license, etc.

minimum_requirements.label: refund/support policy
minimum_requirements.question: How are refund requests and access issues handled?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Buyers need a basic fairness and support policy.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Refund rule and one support path.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business that sells downloadable or digital goods.

minimum_requirements.label: learner promise
minimum_requirements.question: What concrete skill or transformation does the program deliver?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The learning outcome anchors the curriculum.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One measurable or observable learner result.

minimum_requirements.label: learner segment
minimum_requirements.question: Who is the program designed for?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A clear learner type makes pricing, format, and examples easier to choose.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One primary learner profile and what stage they are at.

minimum_requirements.label: delivery format
minimum_requirements.question: How is teaching delivered?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The format determines tools, schedule, and support needs.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Live, cohort, recorded, hybrid, one-to-one, or group.

minimum_requirements.label: curriculum scope
minimum_requirements.question: What minimum modules or sessions are needed to deliver the promise?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The MVP should teach one thing well, not cover everything.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: A short module/session map.

minimum_requirements.label: trainer capacity
minimum_requirements.question: Who teaches and how many learners can they handle well?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Capacity affects quality and scheduling.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Trainer role, maximum class size, and support help if needed.

minimum_requirements.label: join and payment flow
minimum_requirements.question: How do learners register and pay?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Enrollment must be simple and tied to access.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Form, payment rule, confirmation, and onboarding message.

minimum_requirements.label: learner support
minimum_requirements.question: How can learners ask questions and get help?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Support strongly affects perceived value.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Office hours, group chat, email, or Q&A sessions.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business that teaches skills or helps people improve through lessons, programs, or coaching.

minimum_requirements.label: starter catalog
minimum_requirements.question: What products are sold in the first version?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A smaller catalog is easier to manage and explain.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Product range, variants, and what is intentionally excluded from launch.

minimum_requirements.label: product page schema
minimum_requirements.question: What must every product page show?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Users need enough info to buy without asking basic questions.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Name, images, price, stock, description, delivery delay, and variant info.

minimum_requirements.label: stock source
minimum_requirements.question: Where does the product availability truth come from?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Retail breaks when stock is not trusted.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Inventory owner, update method, and low-stock handling.

minimum_requirements.label: checkout flow
minimum_requirements.question: What is the shortest complete order flow?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The MVP must allow a real purchase end to end.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Cart, customer details, payment choice, and confirmation.

minimum_requirements.label: payment options
minimum_requirements.question: Which payment methods are available at launch?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Payment choices affect conversion and operations.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Online payment, COD, transfer, payment link, etc.

minimum_requirements.label: fulfillment routine
minimum_requirements.question: How are orders packed and dispatched?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Selling online without a fulfillment routine creates delays and errors.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Packing step, dispatch timing, and handoff to courier or pickup.

minimum_requirements.label: returns and support
minimum_requirements.question: How do customers ask about orders, returns, or wrong items?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Post-purchase trust matters in retail.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One support channel and one return/exchange rule.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business that sells physical products directly to customers.

minimum_requirements.label: listing type
minimum_requirements.question: What kind of businesses or people are listed?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A directory must be narrow enough to feel useful.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One listing category and one search intent.

minimum_requirements.label: listing fields
minimum_requirements.question: What minimum data must every listing contain?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Without a standard schema, the directory becomes hard to compare.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Name, service, city, contact method, short description, and one trust field.

minimum_requirements.label: discovery flow
minimum_requirements.question: How do visitors find the right listing?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Search and filtering are the main product.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Browse, search, filters, sorting, and location logic.

minimum_requirements.label: lead action
minimum_requirements.question: What exact action turns a visitor into a lead?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The directory only creates business value if it produces leads.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Call, form submission, quote request, WhatsApp click, or booking intent.

minimum_requirements.label: lead form
minimum_requirements.question: What minimum information is captured from the visitor?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Too many fields reduce conversion; too few reduce lead quality.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Contact details and need description only.

minimum_requirements.label: lead routing
minimum_requirements.question: Where does the lead go after submission?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Lead routing defines how value reaches the provider or internal sales team.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Direct provider handoff, internal queue, or both.

minimum_requirements.label: listing review
minimum_requirements.question: How do you stop fake, duplicate, or low-quality listings?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Directory trust depends on curation.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Basic review rule and removal policy.

blueprints.product_type_description: A site that lists businesses or opportunities and captures customer inquiries.

minimum_requirements.label: service menu
minimum_requirements.question: What exact services are sold first?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A local service MVP needs a clear offer, not a vague promise.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: A short menu of services and what each includes.

minimum_requirements.label: service area
minimum_requirements.question: What exact area can you reliably cover?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Location drives cost, quality, and response time.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: City, districts, or radius covered.

minimum_requirements.label: request channel
minimum_requirements.question: How does a customer ask for the service?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Customers need one easy path to start.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Phone, WhatsApp, website form, or booking page.

minimum_requirements.label: pricing rule
minimum_requirements.question: How is the service priced?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A service product is incomplete if the price logic is vague.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Fixed package, hourly rate, or inspection-based price.

minimum_requirements.label: delivery capacity
minimum_requirements.question: Who performs the service and how much work can be handled?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Capacity planning prevents overpromising.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: People, hours, and number of jobs per day/week.

minimum_requirements.label: delivery workflow
minimum_requirements.question: What are the internal steps from request to completed job?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: This is the operational heart of the business.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Receive request, confirm time, deliver, collect payment, close job.

minimum_requirements.label: equipment needed
minimum_requirements.question: What tools or materials are required to deliver well?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Service quality often depends on having the right equipment consistently.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Transport, equipment, consumables, uniforms, etc.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business that provides a service in a specific local area.

minimum_requirements.label: urgent need served
minimum_requirements.question: What near-immediate problem is being solved?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: On-demand products must serve a time-sensitive need.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One same-day or urgent service category.

minimum_requirements.label: provider availability
minimum_requirements.question: Who can fulfill requests and how do you know they are available?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: On-demand systems fail without a live availability model.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Provider pool and availability tracking method.

minimum_requirements.label: request intake
minimum_requirements.question: How does the user request the service?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The request path must be fast and simple.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One instant request channel and what details are required.

minimum_requirements.label: dispatch rule
minimum_requirements.question: How are requests assigned?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Assignment speed determines service quality.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Nearest provider, first available, or zone-based assignment.

minimum_requirements.label: response target
minimum_requirements.question: How fast must the user receive acknowledgment and assignment?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The promise of on-demand depends on response time.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Response SLA and assignment target.

minimum_requirements.label: status visibility
minimum_requirements.question: What statuses can the user and team see?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Real-time visibility reduces support issues.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Simple status set from request to completion.

minimum_requirements.label: failure policy
minimum_requirements.question: What happens if no provider is available or service quality fails?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: On-demand operations need a fallback path.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Reassignment, cancellation, refund, or delay communication rule.

blueprints.product_type_description: A service customers request when they need it, often expecting quick response.

minimum_requirements.label: client outcome
minimum_requirements.question: What result does the agency produce for the client?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Clients pay for a result, not for broad effort.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One clear client outcome and its scope.

minimum_requirements.label: service scope
minimum_requirements.question: What is included and excluded?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Done-for-you services become chaotic without scope boundaries.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Deliverables, exclusions, revision rule, and approval points.

minimum_requirements.label: target client
minimum_requirements.question: Who is the ideal client for launch?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The first client segment shapes delivery and sales.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One client type and why they need the service.

minimum_requirements.label: onboarding inputs
minimum_requirements.question: What must the client provide before work starts?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: You cannot deliver without the right client inputs and access.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Assets, access, goals, approval contact, and deadlines.

minimum_requirements.label: delivery workflow
minimum_requirements.question: What are the internal steps you repeat for every client?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A repeatable workflow is what makes the agency operable.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Intake, execution, review, delivery, and reporting.

minimum_requirements.label: team roles
minimum_requirements.question: Who is needed to deliver the service well?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Role clarity matters even in small teams.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Minimum roles and who owns strategy, production, and client communication.

minimum_requirements.label: billing rule
minimum_requirements.question: How and when does the client pay?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Cash flow and scope control depend on billing structure.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Retainer, project fee, milestone payment, and payment timing.

blueprints.product_type_description: A service business that does work for clients as a custom project or recurring service.

minimum_requirements.label: fixed offer
minimum_requirements.question: What single service package is sold the same way to every customer?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The point of productized service is standardization.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One offer name, scope, and deliverable.

minimum_requirements.label: deliverable
minimum_requirements.question: What does the customer actually receive?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The output must be easy to imagine and evaluate.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One concrete file, report, design, review, or implementation.

minimum_requirements.label: order intake
minimum_requirements.question: What information must the customer provide before work starts?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The intake defines whether delivery can be standardized.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Minimum form fields and attachments.

minimum_requirements.label: delivery time
minimum_requirements.question: What is the guaranteed turnaround?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Predictable delivery time is part of the offer itself.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One promised timeline.

minimum_requirements.label: revision policy
minimum_requirements.question: What changes are included after delivery?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Without a revision rule, the service turns back into custom work.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Number of revisions or follow-up clarification allowed.

minimum_requirements.label: capacity limit
minimum_requirements.question: How many orders can be active at one time?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Standardized services still need workload control.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Maximum active orders or weekly slots.

minimum_requirements.label: payment timing
minimum_requirements.question: When is the customer charged?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Most productized services work best with simple upfront payment.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Before work, deposit, or milestone.

blueprints.product_type_description: A service packaged in a fixed, repeatable format with clear scope and pricing.

minimum_requirements.label: expertise area
minimum_requirements.question: What expertise is being sold?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Expert services need a clear domain to feel credible.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One subject area and one client problem.

minimum_requirements.label: offer format
minimum_requirements.question: What form does the consulting take?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The product should be one defined expert engagement.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Session, audit, memo, retainer, workshop, etc.

minimum_requirements.label: client type
minimum_requirements.question: Who is the consulting for?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Client fit affects scope, language, and price.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One client segment.

minimum_requirements.label: proof of credibility
minimum_requirements.question: What evidence justifies trusting the expert?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Expert services sell trust first.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Case study, years of experience, testimonials, previous results, credentials.

minimum_requirements.label: diagnosis step
minimum_requirements.question: How do you understand the client problem before giving advice?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Consulting without intake leads to weak or generic advice.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Questionnaire, discovery call, or doc review.

minimum_requirements.label: deliverable
minimum_requirements.question: What does the client receive after the consulting engagement?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The output should be useful even after the call ends.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Action plan, memo, recording, roadmap, checklist, or workshop summary.

minimum_requirements.label: follow-up and payment
minimum_requirements.question: What support is included after delivery and when does the client pay?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: This sets both scope and cash flow.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Payment timing and any limited follow-up support.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business that sells expert advice, diagnosis, or recommendations.

minimum_requirements.label: managed function
minimum_requirements.question: What recurring client operation do you run on their behalf?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: This is the core of the service.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One business function with clear boundaries.

minimum_requirements.label: scope boundary
minimum_requirements.question: What tasks do you own and what stays with the client?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Managed services fail when ownership is unclear.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Included tasks, excluded tasks, escalation rules.

minimum_requirements.label: client onboarding
minimum_requirements.question: What access, SOPs, and contacts are needed before service starts?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Operations cannot be managed without setup.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Tools access, rules, templates, and escalation contacts.

minimum_requirements.label: delivery workflow
minimum_requirements.question: What repeatable process is used daily or weekly?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A managed service is really an operating routine.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Step-by-step daily work process.

minimum_requirements.label: service level target
minimum_requirements.question: What response time or quality standard is promised?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Clients need a reason to trust outsourced operations.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Response SLA, quality target, or volume target.

minimum_requirements.label: coverage plan
minimum_requirements.question: Who covers the work and during what hours?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Operational coverage affects staffing and client expectations.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Roles, shifts, and backup coverage.

minimum_requirements.label: reporting and billing
minimum_requirements.question: What does the client see regularly and how do they pay?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Operations providers need regular reporting and clear commercial terms.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Weekly or monthly report and billing structure.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business that takes over an ongoing operational function for clients.

minimum_requirements.label: deal enabled
minimum_requirements.question: What exact deal do you help happen?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Intermediary businesses need one transaction type to optimize around.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One deal type and success event.

minimum_requirements.label: side A supply
minimum_requirements.question: Who is the first side of the market you can source?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: You need supply on at least one side to get started.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Type of seller/provider/owner/expert and how you reach them.

minimum_requirements.label: side B demand
minimum_requirements.question: Who is the second side of the market you can source?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Demand determines qualification and monetization.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Type of buyer/client and what they need.

minimum_requirements.label: qualification criteria
minimum_requirements.question: How do you decide whether a lead is worth introducing?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Lead quality is the core quality control in brokerage.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Budget, readiness, volume, geography, seriousness, etc.

minimum_requirements.label: introduction workflow
minimum_requirements.question: What are the steps from lead intake to successful match?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The business is operationally a deal pipeline.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Intake, qualification, shortlist, introduction, follow-up, closure.

minimum_requirements.label: verification rule
minimum_requirements.question: What minimum checks happen before an introduction?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Trust and liability depend on this.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Identity, business status, proof of inventory/ownership, or provider credibility.

minimum_requirements.label: fee trigger
minimum_requirements.question: When do you get paid?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The business model depends on a clear fee event.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Signed deal, completed service, upfront referral fee, or retained service.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business that connects two sides of a deal and earns a fee or commission.

minimum_requirements.label: starter product line
minimum_requirements.question: What goods will you buy and resell first?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A focused range is easier to source and sell.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One product family and key variants.

minimum_requirements.label: supplier setup
minimum_requirements.question: Who supplies the goods and under what terms?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Supply reliability matters more than having many SKUs.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Supplier names or types, MOQ, lead time, payment terms.

minimum_requirements.label: buyer segment
minimum_requirements.question: Who are the main buyers?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The buyer type changes packaging, delivery, and pricing.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Retail buyers, businesses, resellers, or direct consumers.

minimum_requirements.label: price and margin rule
minimum_requirements.question: How do you turn cost into profitable resale price?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The business dies if margin logic is vague.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Cost components and target margin.

minimum_requirements.label: stock handling
minimum_requirements.question: Where is stock stored and how is it tracked?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Inventory is one of the core operational needs in distribution.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Storage location, count method, and reorder rule.

minimum_requirements.label: order workflow
minimum_requirements.question: What happens from order receipt to dispatch?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The business needs a repeatable order routine.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Confirm stock, confirm order, pack, invoice, dispatch.

minimum_requirements.label: payment rule
minimum_requirements.question: When do buyers pay?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Working-capital pressure depends on this.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Upfront, COD, invoice later, or mixed terms.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business that buys products from producers or suppliers and resells them to others.

minimum_requirements.label: bulk offer
minimum_requirements.question: What products are sold in volume to business buyers?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Wholesale should start with a focused bulk catalog.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Main SKUs, pack sizes, and target buyer type.

minimum_requirements.label: supplier base
minimum_requirements.question: Who keeps your wholesale catalog stocked?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Supply stability matters more than catalog size.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Main suppliers and reorder assumptions.

minimum_requirements.label: business buyer
minimum_requirements.question: What type of business buys from you regularly?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Wholesale operations depend on repeat demand from defined buyer types.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Restaurant, shop, reseller, institution, etc.

minimum_requirements.label: tiered pricing
minimum_requirements.question: How do prices change by quantity or buyer type?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Wholesale is usually driven by order size and account type.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Volume thresholds and price logic.

minimum_requirements.label: warehouse process
minimum_requirements.question: How is bulk inventory stored and picked?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Warehouse routine is a core MVP need for wholesale.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Shelf system, order prep method, and stock count rule.

minimum_requirements.label: order intake
minimum_requirements.question: How do business buyers place orders?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Wholesale often uses low-tech but repeatable ordering channels.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Sales rep, phone, WhatsApp, order form, or portal.

minimum_requirements.label: delivery and payment terms
minimum_requirements.question: How are orders delivered and when do buyers pay?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: These two rules shape cash flow and route planning.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Delivery method and payment term.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business that sells goods in bulk, usually to other businesses rather than end customers.

minimum_requirements.label: goods traded
minimum_requirements.question: What product category is moved across borders?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Cross-border businesses work best when the product scope is narrow at first.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One traded goods category.

minimum_requirements.label: foreign counterparty
minimum_requirements.question: Who is on the foreign side of the transaction?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Trade flow requires a known supplier or buyer abroad.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Supplier or buyer type, location, and role.

minimum_requirements.label: local counterparty
minimum_requirements.question: Who is on the Tunisian side of the transaction?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: You need clarity on who receives, pays, or resells locally.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Importer, distributor, retailer, or industrial buyer.

minimum_requirements.label: commercial terms
minimum_requirements.question: How are pricing, currency, quantity, and payment structured?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Trade viability depends on commercial terms being explicit.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Quantity, currency, payment timing, and delivery term assumptions.

minimum_requirements.label: document set
minimum_requirements.question: What documents are needed for one shipment?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Import/export businesses live on paperwork as much as product.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Invoice, packing list, transport document, customs documentation, etc.

minimum_requirements.label: transport and customs workflow
minimum_requirements.question: How do goods move from supplier to final receiver?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: This is the operating backbone of the business.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Shipping mode, customs handling, and local handoff.

minimum_requirements.label: landed cost model
minimum_requirements.question: How do you calculate the real cost after freight, duties, and local delivery?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Gross margin is impossible to trust without landed cost.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Product cost + logistics + duties + local costs.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business that buys goods from one country and sells them in another.

minimum_requirements.label: product made
minimum_requirements.question: What exact product will be produced first?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Manufacturing should start with one well-defined product line.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One product family and its main variants.

minimum_requirements.label: production method
minimum_requirements.question: How is the product made?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The production process defines equipment, labor, and capacity.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Manual, batch production, assembly, partner factory, etc.

minimum_requirements.label: input sourcing
minimum_requirements.question: What raw materials or parts are required and where do they come from?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Production fails if inputs are unstable or unknown.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Input list, supplier type, and critical dependencies.

minimum_requirements.label: capacity and bottleneck
minimum_requirements.question: How much can you produce and what limits output?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Capacity planning prevents missed demand or wasted stock.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Units per day/week and the main production bottleneck.

minimum_requirements.label: quality check
minimum_requirements.question: How do you know the finished product is good enough to sell?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Manufacturing needs a minimum quality control rule even in an MVP.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Inspection rule, batch check, or acceptance criteria.

minimum_requirements.label: packaging and storage
minimum_requirements.question: How are finished goods packed and stored?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Packaging and storage affect quality and sellability.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Packaging format, labeling, and storage conditions.

minimum_requirements.label: sales route
minimum_requirements.question: How will produced goods be sold first?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Production without a clear sales path is risky.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Direct-to-consumer, wholesale, retail partners, or mixed.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business that makes the product itself instead of only reselling it.

minimum_requirements.label: device job
minimum_requirements.question: What real-world job does the hardware perform?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The hardware product should solve one physical problem clearly.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One device purpose and user outcome.

minimum_requirements.label: minimum device spec
minimum_requirements.question: What is the smallest usable hardware version?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The MVP should define what hardware is essential versus optional.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Main components, power source, and what the first version can do.

minimum_requirements.label: paired software
minimum_requirements.question: What app, dashboard, or backend is required to make the device useful?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Most hardware MVPs are hardware plus software, not hardware alone.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Minimum admin/dashboard/app capabilities.

minimum_requirements.label: setup flow
minimum_requirements.question: How is the device installed, activated, and made usable for the customer?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Installation is often part of the product experience.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Install steps, who performs them, and customer handoff.

minimum_requirements.label: connectivity rule
minimum_requirements.question: How does the device connect and what happens when it goes offline?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Connectivity assumptions affect reliability and support cost.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Wi-Fi/cellular/Bluetooth and offline behavior.

minimum_requirements.label: maintenance path
minimum_requirements.question: How are device faults, updates, or replacements handled?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Hardware businesses need a basic support model at launch.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Support process, replacement rule, and maintenance responsibility.

minimum_requirements.label: safety and warranty baseline
minimum_requirements.question: What minimum safety, handling, and warranty rules apply?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Customers need clear responsibility and trust rules for physical products.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Safe use limits, warranty duration, and misuse exclusions.

blueprints.product_type_description: A physical product with electronics, sensors, or connectivity, usually linked to software.

minimum_requirements.label: shipment types
minimum_requirements.question: What items or goods are you willing to transport first?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A focused shipment scope is easier to price and operate.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Parcels, food orders, documents, ecommerce packages, etc.

minimum_requirements.label: delivery resources
minimum_requirements.question: Who delivers and with what vehicles?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Fleet and courier availability define service capability.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Courier count, vehicle types, and working hours.

minimum_requirements.label: service area
minimum_requirements.question: What exact geographic area is covered at launch?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Geography drives route design and service promise.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Cities, districts, or route zones.

minimum_requirements.label: pickup workflow
minimum_requirements.question: How does a shipment enter the system?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Pickup is the start of the operational chain.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Merchant request, courier notification, collection confirmation.

minimum_requirements.label: dispatch rule
minimum_requirements.question: How are deliveries assigned?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Dispatch is a core operating decision.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Nearest vehicle, zone batching, route planning, etc.

minimum_requirements.label: tracking and proof
minimum_requirements.question: What statuses are visible and how is delivery confirmed?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Delivery businesses need operational traceability.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Shipment statuses and proof-of-delivery method.

minimum_requirements.label: exception handling
minimum_requirements.question: What happens when delivery fails, is delayed, or gets lost?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Delivery operations need a recovery process.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Retry rule, return-to-sender rule, and issue logging.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business that moves goods from one place to another for customers or merchants.

minimum_requirements.label: asset offered
minimum_requirements.question: What item, equipment, or space is rented?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The rental offer must be concrete and trackable.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One asset type and basic inventory definition.

minimum_requirements.label: inventory tracking
minimum_requirements.question: How do you track each asset and its condition?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Rental businesses live on asset visibility.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Asset ID, condition status, and current availability.

minimum_requirements.label: availability system
minimum_requirements.question: How do you know when an asset is free to rent?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Customers need a reliable reservation promise.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Calendar or booking table by asset.

minimum_requirements.label: rental terms
minimum_requirements.question: What are the core rental rules?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Terms protect the asset and set customer expectations.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Duration, price, deposit, late fee, and usage limits.

minimum_requirements.label: reservation flow
minimum_requirements.question: How does a customer reserve the asset?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The MVP needs a complete reserve-to-handover flow.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Inquiry, confirmation, payment/deposit, pickup details.

minimum_requirements.label: handover and return check
minimum_requirements.question: What is checked when the asset is given out and returned?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Condition control is a core rental process.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Identity check, condition check, return inspection.

minimum_requirements.label: damage and maintenance rule
minimum_requirements.question: How are repairs, damage, and downtime handled?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Rental margins disappear without a damage policy.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Deposit usage, repair flow, and asset unavailability rule.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business that gives temporary access to an asset instead of selling it.

minimum_requirements.label: repair scope
minimum_requirements.question: What items or systems do you repair in the MVP?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A narrow scope improves speed, expertise, and marketing clarity.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One repair category and what is excluded.

minimum_requirements.label: job intake
minimum_requirements.question: How do customers submit a repair request?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Jobs need a standard intake path to be priced and tracked.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Walk-in, call, message, or form plus required details.

minimum_requirements.label: diagnosis rule
minimum_requirements.question: How do you inspect and confirm the issue?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A repair business needs a standard diagnosis step before promising a price.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Inspection method, timing, and whether it is paid.

minimum_requirements.label: quote and approval
minimum_requirements.question: How do customers approve repair work?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Repairs often fail commercially when approval is not explicit.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Price quote, parts estimate, and customer approval rule.

minimum_requirements.label: parts sourcing
minimum_requirements.question: Where do parts or consumables come from?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Repair speed and margin depend on parts availability.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Main suppliers and special-order rule.

minimum_requirements.label: repair workflow
minimum_requirements.question: What are the internal steps from intake to completion?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: This is the operating backbone of the service.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Receive item, diagnose, quote, repair, test, notify customer.

minimum_requirements.label: payment and warranty
minimum_requirements.question: When does the customer pay and what guarantee is offered?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Customers expect both a payment rule and post-repair confidence.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Payment timing and warranty period/scope.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business that fixes, maintains, or services equipment, devices, or property.

minimum_requirements.label: repeatable offer
minimum_requirements.question: What customer offer must work the same way in every branch?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Franchise models depend on repeatability more than novelty.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One standard service/product experience.

minimum_requirements.label: branch unit spec
minimum_requirements.question: What does one branch need to open and operate?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A franchise needs a clear branch formula.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Location type, size, minimum staff, equipment, and initial stock.

minimum_requirements.label: operating manual
minimum_requirements.question: What standard procedures must every branch follow?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Branch consistency comes from standard procedures.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Opening, customer service, hygiene, ordering, closing, and issue handling.

minimum_requirements.label: training package
minimum_requirements.question: How do new branch operators learn the model?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: The business cannot expand without teachable operations.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Initial training topics and support period.

minimum_requirements.label: brand control
minimum_requirements.question: How do you keep experience and quality consistent?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Customers expect the same brand everywhere.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Visual standards, service standards, and audit rule.

minimum_requirements.label: supply chain rule
minimum_requirements.question: How do branches get approved products or materials?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Supply consistency protects margin and brand quality.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Approved suppliers, central buying, or mandatory materials.

minimum_requirements.label: expansion gate
minimum_requirements.question: What must be true before opening another branch?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Scaling too early breaks franchise models.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Financial and operational threshold for the next location.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business model where the same brand and operating system is repeated across branches or operators.

minimum_requirements.label: experience sold
minimum_requirements.question: What exact event or experience is being offered?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: An event product must feel tangible before it is purchased.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One event concept, duration, and value promise.

minimum_requirements.label: attendee segment
minimum_requirements.question: Who is the event for?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Audience fit affects price, promotion, and format.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: One attendee type and why they would care.

minimum_requirements.label: format and venue
minimum_requirements.question: Where and how does the event happen?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Venue/platform defines cost and operations.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Physical venue, online platform, or hybrid format.

minimum_requirements.label: run of show
minimum_requirements.question: What is the program flow from start to finish?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Events need an agenda to be deliverable.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Start time, sections, breaks, and end result.

minimum_requirements.label: capacity and staffing
minimum_requirements.question: How many attendees can be handled and by whom?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Capacity planning protects event quality.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Seat limit and minimum team roles.

minimum_requirements.label: booking and payment
minimum_requirements.question: How do people reserve and pay for attendance?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: A complete booking path is required for launch.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Event page, checkout, confirmation, and payment timing.

minimum_requirements.label: materials and follow-up
minimum_requirements.question: What must be ready before the event and what happens after it?
minimum_requirements.why_it_matters: Experience quality depends on setup and post-event follow-up.
minimum_requirements.minimum_answer_should_include: Materials checklist, setup plan, and post-event action.

blueprints.product_type_description: A business that organizes or sells participation in events, activities, or experiences.
